content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

BAGATELOS & FADEM
THE INTERNATIONAL BUILDING
601 CALIFORNIA STREET
SUITE 1801
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108

BARRY FADEM
PETER A. BAGATELOS
WES VAN WINKLE

FPPC
May 3 12:01 PM '90

TELEPHONE
(415) 982-7100
FAX
(415) 982-1085

April 27, 1990


Ms. Alice Hughes
Technical Advice and Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

     Re:  Confirmation of Telephone Advice

Dear Ms. Hughes:

     This firms serves as legal counsel to Frank Caterinicchio,
the chief executive officer of a registered slate mailer
organization.  On Mr. Caterinicchio's behalf, I am writing to
confirm advice that I received from you this morning by
telephone.

     As you will recall, I spoke to you by telephone on April 24,
1990, to inquire whether Mr. Caterinicchio's organization would
be required to file separate campaign reports for each of three
slate mailers which Mr. Caterinicchio proposes to prepare and
send in connection with the June primary election.  Although
each of the slate mailers will bear a different slate
registered state mailer organization name will be disclosed on
each slate mailer in the manner required by Government Code
Section 84305.5.  In addition, certain campaign committees may
wish to appear on more than one of the slate mailers; and I
therefore inquired whether the Form 401 filed by the
organization would need to disclose the amounts paid to
such committee for each mailer, or whether the report could
simply disclose a single payment from a committee for multiple
slate mailers.  You indicated that you would discuss these
questions with other commission staff members and would respond
the following day.

     You informed me by telephone this morning that the staff's
consensus was that the organization would only be required to
file a single campaign report for the three slate mailers and
would not be required to register as three different slate
mailer organizations.  In addition, you indicated that Form 401
merely requires disclosure of each committee which makes a
payment to appear on one or more slate mailers and does not
require disclosure of the amounts paid for each individual
slate mailer.

Ms. Alice Hughes
April 27, 1990
Page Two

     Thank you for your assistance on this matter.  In the event
that I have misunderstood or inadvertently misstated any of your
advice, please contact me immediately at this office.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Wes Van Winkle
                                        Of Counsel


WWW/map
abc:4-251tr

FPPC    LAW OFFICES OF
        BAGATELOS & FADEM
BARRY FADEM            THE INTERNATIONAL BUILDING                    TELEPHONE
PETER A. BAGATELOS     601 CALIFORNIA STREET                         (415) 982-7100
WES VAN WINKLE         SUITE 1801                                    FAX
                       SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108               (415) 982-1085

                              April 26, 1990


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

     Re:  Confirmation of Advice re Slate Mailer Organizations

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

     This firm serves as legal counsel to Mr. Frank
Cateriniccio, the chief executive officer of a slate mailer
organization.  On behalf of Mr. Cateriniccio, I am writing to
confirm telephone advice which I received from you earlier
today.

     As you recall, I explained that many slate mailer
organizations charge different amounts to different candidates
who appear on the same slate mailer, and sometimes place a
particular candidate on a slate mailer at no charge because the
presence of that particular candidate on the slate mailer
attracts other candidate to the slate.  Therefore, the
candidates who appear on the slate mailer at no charge, or at a
reduced rate, receive a benefit from the paying candidates who
appear on the slate.  For this reason, I inquired whether the
Fair Political Practices Commission construes such transactions
as violating the ban on transfers between candidates or the
spending limits imposed by Proposition 73.

     You indicated that the staff has taken the position that
unless a candidate specifically intends his or her payment to a
slate mailer organization to benefit another candidate or
candidates appearing on the slate, arrangements such as these
are not considered to violate the ban on transfers between
candidates.  Moreover, because the slate mailer organization is
profit-motivated and deals with each candidate separately in
arms-length transaction, candidates are often unaware that their
payments to a particular slate mailer organization may have the
effect of benefiting another candidate or candidates.

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
April 26, 1990
Page Two

     I indicated to you that because I would be seeking formal
written advice with regard to a number of other questions
pertaining to slate mailers, I would also include this question
in that request.

     Thank you for your assistance in this matter. In the event
that I have inadvertently misstated or misunderstood any of your
advice, please contact me immediately. Please do not hesitate
to contact me if there is any additional information I can
provide.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Wes Van Winkle
                                        Of Counsel

WVW/map

You informed me by telephone this morning that the staff's consensus was that the organization would only be required to file a single campaign report for the three slate mailers and would not be required to register as three different slate mailer organizations. In addition, you indicated that Form 401 merely requires disclosure of each committee which makes a payment to appear on one or more slate mailers and does not require disclosure of the amounts paid for each individual slate mailer.
